reasoning: D builds part (a) two genuinely independent ways (Fermi demand chain plus BLS occupational base rate) and explicitly reconciles them at order of magnitude, while handling the city-vs-metro ambiguity transparently and pinning the part (b) framing trap hardest ('not 100 minus the business share'); its calibration width is justified by definitional uncertainty rather than ignorance. A is close behind with the same binary-trap catch, MBA confound, and a real PTG cross-check, but uses only one demand path and its tunings-per-tuner step is muddled. C is the most empirically grounded, citing actual figures (BLS 150 metro repairers/tuners with the self-employed-exclusion caveat, engineering 19.2% / econ 11.8% CEO studies), but its city/metro adjustment chain is opaque and it surfaces fewer cognitive traps. B's demand build is inflated (1.3 blended tunings/piano pushing to ~310 FTE) and it does the least framing work on the non-exhaustive bucket question.
